description: Productize ReAgent into a usable v1 for individual researchers with a stronger first-use loop, more trustworthy research outputs, durable backup and restore flows, and release-grade browser smoke checks.

userStories.title: Add onboarding readiness summary
userStories.description: As a first-time individual researcher, I want `reagent onboard` to tell me what is blocked and what to do next so that I can start the product without reading the whole repo.
acceptanceCriteria: Classify onboarding checks into required and optional items
Readiness output covers `.env`, workspace, database, LLM route, channel mode, and runtime port
Each blocking issue includes one explicit next step
`--json` output is stable and machine-readable
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Apply a safe starter profile during onboarding
userStories.description: As a first-time individual researcher, I want onboarding to apply a safe starter profile so that I can reach the product loop before configuring final providers.
acceptanceCriteria: `reagent onboard --apply` can set a safe `fallback + mock` starter profile when provider config is missing
Existing explicit provider values are not overwritten
After onboarding apply, `reagent home` can load without additional manual setup
Onboarding output explains that the starter profile is for evaluation rather than final research quality
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add first-use checklist to the home and landing surfaces
userStories.description: As a first-time individual researcher, I want the home and landing pages to tell me the next step so that I can reach my first useful report from an empty workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: Home or landing shows a first useful report checklist
Checklist includes create brief, run research or discovery, open report, and save memory
Each checklist item includes a direct action or navigation target
Checklist reflects completed state when the workspace already has matching artifacts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add brief starter templates for individual researchers
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want starter brief templates so that I do not begin every new topic from an empty form.
acceptanceCriteria: Web offers at least three brief starter templates for personal research
Each template includes topic, target problem, success criteria, known baselines, and evaluation priorities
A selected template can be edited and saved as a direction or brief
Brief import and export still work after template support is added
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Improve discovery normalization and candidate deduplication
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want repeated or near-duplicate discovery candidates to be suppressed so that I spend time on distinct papers rather than noisy repeats.
acceptanceCriteria: Discovery candidate generation suppresses exact and near-duplicate paper candidates
Returned candidates keep stable source metadata for later UI display
When deduplication removes candidates, the run still returns deterministic ordering
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Show discovery ranking reasons and candidate context in the UI
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want discovery results to explain why each candidate is ranked so that I can quickly decide what to read next.
acceptanceCriteria: Discovery result cards show ranking reasons when available
Each candidate card shows source, year, and venue or equivalent metadata when available
Weak-result and empty-result states show explanatory copy rather than a blank list
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Surface evidence, support labels, and warnings in report detail
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want report detail to distinguish evidence-backed claims from inference so that I can judge whether a result is trustworthy.
acceptanceCriteria: Report detail shows evidence items with source type and confidence
The UI distinguishes paper-supported, code-supported, or inference-backed conclusions when data is available
Weak evidence coverage or high inference proportion shows an explicit warning state
No-evidence reports show a clear empty state instead of silently omitting evidence sections
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add recent artifact reopen flow for personal work loops
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want to reopen my latest report, presentation, module asset, or workstream memo so that prior work stays part of the daily loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Web shows recent artifacts including report, presentation, module asset, and workstream memo when present
Recent artifact items navigate directly to a detail view or file-backed content view
`reagent home` surfaces the latest report and latest active task
Missing artifact targets return a readable error instead of a raw path failure
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Export a single research artifact bundle
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want to export one research result as a reusable bundle so that I can share or archive it outside the workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a command that exports a single artifact bundle
The bundle can include report, review, related workstream memo, and core metadata
Successful export returns a concrete local file path
Export failures explain which required files are missing
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create workspace backup snapshots
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want one command to back up the whole workspace so that I can safely upgrade, migrate, or experiment without losing work.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a workspace backup snapshot command
Backup covers file-backed memory, channel runtime state, research rounds, and the database layer
Backup output returns one snapshot path
Backup does not overwrite an existing snapshot by default
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preview workspace restore before applying it
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want to preview a backup before restore so that I do not accidentally replace my current workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a restore preview command
Preview shows snapshot timestamp, included content, and target workspace path
Preview does not modify the active workspace
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Apply workspace restore safely
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want to apply a restore with explicit confirmation so that I can recover a workspace without silent data loss.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a restore apply command
Restore apply requires explicit confirmation or an equivalent safety flag
After restore, `reagent home`, recent research, and memory files can load restored data
Restore failures leave the current workspace in a debuggable state with a clear error message
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add product-facing recovery hints to key surfaces
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want product-facing recovery hints on blocked surfaces so that I know how to recover without reading raw logs first.
acceptanceCriteria: Home, settings, channels, and agents surfaces show product-facing recovery hints for blocking failures
Recovery hints are actionable and name the next command or setting to check
Hints do not expose raw internal stack traces by default
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Export a support bundle without leaking secrets
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want to export a support bundle so that I can debug or ask for help without manually collecting logs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a support bundle export command
The bundle includes runtime status, doctor summary, recent logs, channel lifecycle, and agent runtime audit
Plaintext secrets are excluded by default
Successful export returns one local bundle path
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add a daily digest preset for individual researchers
userStories.description: As an individual researcher, I want a daily digest preset so that I can enable routine updates without manually composing scheduler fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Web provides a daily digest preset
The preset configures sender, time, topK, max papers, and enabled state
The preset works with the current brief or direction configuration
Scheduler empty and disabled states explain what is missing
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add browser smoke verification to release checks
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want release verification to run a browser smoke flow so that public beta builds cannot ship with broken core pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Release verification runs a browser smoke flow
The smoke flow covers Sessions, Agents, session switching, and history, hooks, runtime, and delegations panels
Smoke failures emit debuggable artifacts such as screenshots or result JSON
Smoke can run against a mock environment without real WeChat pairing
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
